lines.text: Off Anchorage swims one of the rarest whales on Earth. It just got two watchers.
Noah switched on two A.I. systems to find Cook Inlet's belugas.
One listens. A deep learning model reads the noisy inlet and finds a single beluga call.
One looks. Guy-uh scans satellite photos from orbit for the white smudge of a whale.
Ear and eye, the same whale, at once, for the first time.
It matters because only about three thirty one of these belugas are left.
There were thirteen hundred in nineteen seventy nine. A drop of almost eighty percent.
But watching is not saving. Sharper eyes do not quiet the boats or refill the salmon these whales need.
What the A.I. gives Alaska is one hard, open number. Three thirty one.
Whether that becomes a floor to defend, or just a countdown on record, no machine decides.
